title: Milk  supply per person
subtitle: Average per capita milk supply, measured in kilograms per person per year. This includes the milk equivalents of dairy products made from milk ingredients, but excludes butter.
note: Data is based on per capita food supply at the consumer level, but does not account for food waste at the consumer level.
caption:
Milk  supply per person

Average per capita milk supply, measured in kilograms per person per year. This includes the milk equivalents of dairy products made from milk ingredients, but excludes butter.

This choropleth world map displays 2023 average per capita milk supply, measured in kilograms per person per year. The legend ranges from 0 kg to 300 kg, with specific tick points at 50 kg intervals. The darkest blue shades, representing values above 250 kg, are visible in Ireland, Finland, and the Netherlands. Surrounding Western and Northern European countries, along with the United States, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand, sit in the next darkest band, falling between 200 kg and 250 kg. Argentina and Uruguay also appear in this upper-mid range. In contrast, a broad swath of Sub-Saharan Africa, including countries like Nigeria, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and Tanzania, sits in the lightest yellow shades, indicating values below 50 kg. Much of South and Southeast Asia, including India, Indonesia, and the Philippines, also falls within this lowest band. China and Russia appear in mid-range teal shades, situated between 100 kg and 150 kg. Brazil and Mexico show slightly darker shades than their neighbors, placing them in the 100 kg to 150 kg range. Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching, indicating no data is available. The visual pattern shows the highest supply levels concentrated in Europe, North America, and Oceania, while the lowest levels are distributed across much of Africa and Asia. The data reflects per capita food supply at the consumer level but does not account for food waste at that level.
